title: S14-D2 C5 equity-corporate-action contract extension (authoritative phase-2 safety contract for stock splits, reverse splits, dividends, special dividends, symbol changes, mergers, spin-offs, delistings, survivorship bias, adjusted-vs-unadjusted OHLCV handling, and vendor methodology differences for the AAPL/MSFT/NVDA universe; PRECOMMITTED at this turn for by-reference inclusion in S14-D2 SEAL artifact; does NOT authorize fetch, SEAL, BUILD, or any subsequent phase)
report_kind: Phase-2 safety contract extension: defines the C5 corporate-action handling specification for cash-equity candidate s14-d2 (AAPL/MSFT/NVDA basket). Extends the generic C1-C8 phase-2 safety contract framework (which was authored for futures candidates) with equity-specific behavior for splits, dividends, mergers, spin-offs, and delistings. PRECOMMITTED at C5-extension authoring time so that SEAL can reference this contract by-version without re-authoring. Does NOT modify any existing sealed candidate artifact. Does NOT modify DR10 v2. Does NOT roll back T1 (5376de7) PROVISIONAL status. Does NOT authorize data fetch, SEAL, BUILD, P4, P6, P6.5, P7, P10, or P11. Each subsequent step requires separate operator-typed authorization.

scope.extends_framework: Generic C1-C8 phase-2 safety contract framework (futures-authored). This C5 extension adds equity-specific behavior.

stock_splits.definition: A forward stock split increases the number of shares outstanding by a fixed ratio (e.g., 2:1, 4:1, 10:1) at no cost to existing shareholders; per-share price drops proportionally on ex-date.
stock_splits.adjustment_formula: all pre-split prices are divided by the split ratio; volumes are multiplied by the split ratio. Result: continuous price series across split events with no discontinuity in OHLCV.
stock_splits.position_handling_at_ex_date: Existing position size is multiplied by the split ratio at no cost. Example: 100 shares of NVDA held on 2024-06-09 (close of day before ex-date) become 1000 shares of NVDA on 2024-06-10 (ex-date open) with no cash flow. Per-share entry price is divided by the split ratio for P&L attribution.
stock_splits.rsi_signal_continuity: Because sealed CSV uses backward-adjusted prices, RSI(3) signal computation is transparent to split events. The underlying mean-reversion behavior is preserved across splits when computed on the adjusted price series.
stock_splits.audit_requirement: SEAL must precommit a manually-curated split event log for {AAPL, MSFT, NVDA} over the IS+OOS window, cross-referenced against the SEAL-selected data vendor's split records. Any discrepancy must be resolved before SEAL completion.

reverse_splits.definition: A reverse stock split decreases the number of shares outstanding by a fixed ratio (e.g., 1:5, 1:10); per-share price rises proportionally on ex-date.
reverse_splits.expected_in_universe: ZERO. AAPL, MSFT, and NVDA have never executed a reverse split. Reverse splits typically signal distress (low share price compliance with exchange listing rules); none of the three target tickers are reverse-split candidates.
reverse_splits.handling_if_unexpected_event_occurs: Treat symmetrically with forward splits but with ratio inverted: pre-event prices are multiplied by reverse-split ratio (e.g., 1:5 → multiply by 5); volumes divided by ratio. Position-size adjustment: 1000 shares before 1:5 reverse → 200 shares after, with per-share entry price multiplied by 5. RSI signal continuity preserved via backward-adjusted CSV.
reverse_splits.audit_requirement: SEAL must verify ZERO reverse-split events in IS+OOS window for AAPL/MSFT/NVDA. If any reverse-split event is detected, candidate must be re-PLANned with substitute universe.

dividends.definition: Regular periodic cash distributions to shareholders, typically quarterly for US large-caps. Per-share price typically drops by approximately the dividend amount on ex-dividend date.
dividends.cash_flow_handling: Dividend cash received on ex-dividend date is credited to the account cash balance. Dividend is NOT auto-reinvested into additional shares of the paying stock. This matches realistic retail cash-account behavior and avoids dividend-reinvestment look-ahead bias.
dividends.price_series_for_rsi_signal: PRICE-ONLY (not total-return). RSI(3) signal computed on backward-adjusted close prices that are split-adjusted but NOT dividend-adjusted. The price drop on ex-dividend date is a real market-data event and is included in the RSI computation as a legitimate price observation.
dividends.rationale_for_price_only_rsi: Using dividend-adjusted (total-return) prices would smooth out the ex-dividend price drop, making RSI behavior diverge from what an actual trader observing real-time prices would see. Price-only RSI matches reality.
dividends.pnl_attribution: Each closed trade's P&L = price_diff + dividends_received_during_hold. Aggregate P&L tracks both components separately for diagnostic transparency. K9 trade count counts price-event-based trades only (dividends do not generate signal-triggered trades).
dividends.look_ahead_bias_avoidance: Dividend announcements (declared before ex-date) MUST NOT influence signal computation. Signal uses only OHLC + close prices, not announcement metadata.
dividends.audit_requirement: SEAL must precommit a manually-curated dividend event log for {AAPL, MSFT, NVDA} over IS+OOS window (ex-date, amount, regular vs special, frequency), cross-referenced against the SEAL-selected data vendor's dividend records.

special_dividends.definition: One-time large cash distributions outside the regular dividend schedule (e.g., Microsoft 2004 $3/share special dividend; ~$32B total).
special_dividends.treatment: SAME as regular dividends per section 4: cash to account on ex-date; price drop included in RSI signal; P&L attribution tracks separately.
special_dividends.rationale_for_same_treatment: A special dividend is mechanically identical to a regular dividend in market behavior (ex-date cash flow + price adjustment); treating them differently would create ad-hoc rules without principled basis.
special_dividends.expected_in_window: LOW. MSFT 2004 $3 special is pre-IS window. AAPL has not paid a special dividend since dividend reinstatement in 2012. NVDA has not paid a special dividend.
special_dividends.audit_requirement: SEAL must precommit special-dividend event flags within the dividend event log; any special dividend in IS+OOS window must be documented with verbatim per-share amount and ex-date.

symbol_changes.definition: Ticker symbol change due to corporate restructuring, rebranding, or exchange transfer (e.g., GOOG/GOOGL split in 2014; FB → META in 2022).
symbol_changes.expected_in_universe: ZERO. AAPL (since IPO 1980), MSFT (since IPO 1986), and NVDA (since IPO 1999) have all maintained their original ticker symbols and are all listed on NASDAQ. No symbol changes expected in IS+OOS window.
symbol_changes.handling_if_unexpected_event_occurs: Sealed CSV is keyed by historical-ticker-at-bar-date. Symbol-change events documented in event log. RSI signal computation continues across symbol-change boundary using continuous price series.
symbol_changes.audit_requirement: SEAL must verify NO symbol-change events for {AAPL, MSFT, NVDA} in IS+OOS window. If detected, candidate must be re-PLANned with substitute universe.

mergers_acquisitions.definition: Corporate combination where target company shares are exchanged for acquirer shares + cash, or fully cashed out. Can result in delisting of target ticker.
mergers_acquisitions.expected_in_universe: ZERO. AAPL, MSFT, and NVDA are all S&P 100 / Nasdaq 100 acquirers, not acquisition targets. No merger/acquisition of any of the three is expected in IS+OOS window through reasonable forecast horizon.
mergers_acquisitions.handling_per_PLAN_section_6_3: CLOSE_AND_DO_NOT_REPLACE: if AAPL/MSFT/NVDA is acquired and delisted during IS or OOS window, position is closed at last-available bar price; basket shrinks to 2 names; no replacement.
mergers_acquisitions.rationale_for_close_and_do_not_replace: Simpler than maintaining an eligibility-list with substitution rules. Avoids look-ahead bias from eligibility-list construction. The diagnostic value of 'RSI(3) on AAPL/MSFT/NVDA' degrades gracefully if a name is lost rather than being patched with a post-hoc-chosen substitute.
mergers_acquisitions.cash_or_stock_consideration_handling: Cash-buyout: position closed at cash payout price; proceeds credited to account. Stock-merger (target shares exchanged for acquirer shares): proceeds liquidated at first available trading day's open price for the new acquirer; cash credited; no continuing position in the acquirer (basket shrinks to 2 names).
mergers_acquisitions.audit_requirement: SEAL must verify NO merger/acquisition completion events for {AAPL, MSFT, NVDA} during IS+OOS window. If any detected, candidate must be re-PLANned with substitute universe.

spin_offs.definition: Corporate distribution of subsidiary shares to existing shareholders, creating a new tradable entity. Original parent share price drops by approximately the spun-off subsidiary's per-share value on ex-date.
spin_offs.expected_in_universe: ZERO. AAPL, MSFT, and NVDA have no announced spin-off plans. No spin-off events expected in IS+OOS window.
spin_offs.handling_if_unexpected_event_occurs: Spun-off shares are sold immediately at the spin-off subsidiary's first market-open price. Cash proceeds credited to account. Original position in parent remains open at the post-spin-off adjusted price (parent shares not closed). RSI signal computation uses the parent's backward-adjusted price series, which incorporates the spin-off price drop.
spin_offs.audit_requirement: SEAL must verify NO spin-off events for {AAPL, MSFT, NVDA} during IS+OOS window. If any detected, candidate must be re-PLANned.

delistings.definition: Removal of a stock from exchange listing, typically due to merger completion, bankruptcy, or failure to meet listing requirements.
delistings.expected_in_universe: ZERO. AAPL, MSFT, and NVDA are all in stable continuous listing on NASDAQ with no foreseeable delisting risk in IS+OOS window.
delistings.handling_per_PLAN_section_6_3: CLOSE_AND_DO_NOT_REPLACE: position closed at last available bar price; basket shrinks to 2 names; K11-style portfolio cap reduces to 2; no new positions in delisted name.
delistings.cash_payout_at_delisting: If delisting includes cash payout (e.g., merger consideration), cash credited to account at merger-effective date.
delistings.audit_requirement: SEAL must verify NO delisting events for {AAPL, MSFT, NVDA} in IS+OOS window. If any detected, candidate must be re-PLANned with substitute universe.

survivorship_bias.definition: Selection bias arising from choosing currently-thriving names whose past performance was used to justify their selection. The unchosen 'failure cases' (delisted, merged, distressed) are not represented in the diagnostic.
survivorship_bias.bias_direction: UPWARD. AAPL/MSFT/NVDA were selected in 2026 (current). These are all current-thriving large-cap US tech names. IS window 2019-2025 captures their outperformance period. Failure cases (e.g., NOK, BBRY, IBM-old-tech-era) are not represented.
survivorship_bias.nvda_specific_bias: NVDA had AI-driven outperformance 2023-2025 = regime-specific tailwind. RSI(3) mean-reversion behavior on NVDA during this period may reflect the AI-bubble regime rather than generalizable mean-reversion edge.
survivorship_bias.msft_specific_bias: MSFT had cloud + AI transformation tailwind 2019-2025 (Azure growth + OpenAI partnership). Mean-reversion behavior may reflect tech-megacap-outperformance regime.
survivorship_bias.aapl_specific_bias: AAPL had services-growth + buyback tailwind 2019-2025. Less regime-specific than NVDA/MSFT but still currently-thriving-selection bias.
survivorship_bias.mitigation_strategy: EXPLICIT_ACKNOWLEDGMENT_IN_SEAL_AND_P11. Per PLAN §6.6 and DRAFT §9.5, SEAL artifact must include verbatim universe-selection-rationale section acknowledging: (i) sector concentration (all 3 tech); (ii) survivorship bias (current-thriving selection); (iii) NVDA AI-tailwind specificity. P11 lifecycle memo MUST frame diagnostic value as 'did RSI(3) work on THESE 3 names in 2019-2025' (precise question with precise answer), NOT as 'this strategy works on cash equities' (overgeneralization).
survivorship_bias.alternative_mitigation_not_taken_at_PLAN: Rolling eligibility universe (more robust; more complex). PLAN deliberately chose frozen 3-name basket for reproducibility. SEAL may revisit if operator chooses rolling-universe approach at SEAL time.
survivorship_bias.audit_requirement: SEAL artifact's universe-selection-rationale section must be authored verbatim with explicit survivorship-bias acknowledgment (cannot be elided or implicit). P11 memo must frame diagnostic value precisely.

nvda_split_history_risk_explicit.risk_assessment: Both NVDA splits (2021-07-20 4:1 and 2024-06-10 10:1) fall within the plausible IS window (assuming IS spans ~2019-2025 per PLAN §5.1). Effective NVDA split factor across both = 40x (4 × 10). Pre-2021 NVDA prices on raw scale were ~$200-700; post-2024-split adjusted equivalents on a 40x-back-adjusted scale are ~$5-17.50. CRSP-style backward-adjusted CSV will show continuous price series across both events at back-adjusted scale.
nvda_split_history_risk_explicit.audit_requirement_specific_to_nvda: SEAL must verify the SEAL-selected data vendor's NVDA back-adjusted price series includes BOTH the 2021-07-20 4:1 and the 2024-06-10 10:1 splits. Verification method: compare a hand-checked pre-split price (e.g., NVDA 2024-06-07 close, which was approximately $1,209 raw / $120.90 post-2024-split / $120.90 post-2024-and-2021-back-adjusted) against the vendor's reported value. Discrepancy of more than 1% on this specific bar indicates split-adjustment methodology issue requiring re-FETCH or vendor switch.
nvda_split_history_risk_explicit.rsi_signal_impact_of_nvda_splits: ZERO if CRSP back-adjustment is correctly applied. The RSI(3) computation operates on a continuous price series; the split events are mathematically transparent. If back-adjustment is INCORRECT (e.g., one split missed; back-adjustment formula errored), RSI signal will show artificial extreme readings at the split ex-date which would corrupt the diagnostic.
nvda_split_history_risk_explicit.position_size_impact_at_split: Trade-quantity tracking must explicitly handle the position size multiplication: a long position of (e.g.) 50 shares of NVDA opened 2024-06-05 at $1,200 raw and held through 2024-06-10 ex-date becomes 500 shares of NVDA at back-adjusted entry price of $120 with no cash flow. SEAL must precommit that the BUILD runner correctly handles this position-size adjustment.

adjusted_vs_unadjusted_ohlcv.rationale: Splits are pure mathematical adjustments (no economic value change); back-adjustment preserves continuity without contaminating the signal. Dividends are economic events (cash transfer to shareholders); adjusting prices for dividends would smooth out a real market-data event (ex-dividend price drop) that an actual trader observes in real time. RSI computed on dividend-adjusted prices would diverge from RSI computed on observable market data.
adjusted_vs_unadjusted_ohlcv.consequence_for_vendor_selection: If a data vendor's default 'Close' field is dividend-adjusted (e.g., Yahoo Finance / yfinance 'Adj Close' or default 'Close'), the SEAL artifact must either: (a) use the vendor's UNADJUSTED close field (if available); OR (b) reconstruct the unadjusted close from the dividend-adjusted close + dividend event log (verifiable byte-equivalent reconstruction). If neither is feasible, the vendor must be replaced.

specific_vendor_notes.databento_equities: Provides both unadjusted OHLCV and adjusted variants; SEAL should use Databento's unadjusted close + apply CRSP-style split adjustment via Databento's documented split event log. Dividend handling: track separately, not applied to price series.
specific_vendor_notes.polygon: Provides both unadjusted ('open', 'high', 'low', 'close') and adjusted ('o', 'h', 'l', 'c' with adjusted=true parameter) endpoints. SEAL should use the adjusted-for-splits-only variant if available, or apply CRSP-style adjustment to unadjusted data using Polygon's split event log.
specific_vendor_notes.alpaca: Provides split-adjusted OHLCV by default. Verify that Alpaca's default adjustment is splits-only (not dividends). If dividend-adjustment is also applied, must use raw bars + split event log.
specific_vendor_notes.iex_cloud: Historical data endpoints provide both adjusted and unadjusted variants. SEAL must verify IEX Cloud is still operational at SEAL time (sunset announced Aug 2024; check current status).
specific_vendor_notes.yfinance: Yahoo Finance default 'Close' is the un-adjusted close BUT 'Adj Close' is BOTH split- AND dividend-adjusted. yfinance Python library exposes both. SEAL must use 'Close' (unadjusted) and apply CRSP-style split adjustment via separately-fetched split event log. yfinance is the highest-friction vendor for this candidate due to the dual adjustment in 'Adj Close'.

adjusted_vs_unadjusted_ohlcv.audit_requirement: SEAL must document the data-vendor adjustment methodology by URL + version; verify split-only adjustment (no dividend contamination) on a hand-checked pre-split bar for each universe member.

key_axes: Split adjustment timing (T-1 close vs T open at ex-date)
Split-adjustment formula (CRSP backward vs forward vs Yahoo-style)
Dividend treatment (included in default Close vs separate)
Volume adjustment for splits (multiplied by ratio: yes/no)
Corporate-action event log granularity (per-event vs aggregated)
Symbol-change continuity (historical-ticker keyed vs current-ticker keyed)
vendor_methodology_differences.implication_for_seal: Different vendor methodologies produce different back-adjusted price series for the same underlying ticker. SEAL must lock the vendor + methodology version such that byte-equivalent reproduction is feasible from documented primary sources.
vendor_methodology_differences.byte_equivalent_reproducibility_requirem: Sealed CSV must be byte-equivalently re-derivable from: (a) vendor source + version; (b) split event log; (c) dividend event log; (d) any vendor-specific adjustment parameters. SEAL artifact must include all four anchors with sha256 hashes.

required_data_vendor_fields_for_corporat.vendor_must_provide_OR_seal_must_compute: Each minimum field must be either provided directly by the SEAL-selected vendor OR computable from vendor's primary fields with a documented formula. SEAL artifact must specify per-field which mode applies.
required_data_vendor_fields_for_corporat.audit_method: Cross-reference vendor's reported corporate-action events against a second independent source (e.g., SEC EDGAR 8-K filings; company investor-relations announcements) for at least one event of each type (split, dividend, etc.) in IS+OOS window. Document any discrepancy.

adjusted_prices_affecting_rsi_signal.summary: Correctly back-adjusted prices preserve RSI signal behavior across split events. Incorrectly adjusted (or unadjusted) prices corrupt the signal.
adjusted_prices_affecting_rsi_signal.scenario_correctly_split_adjusted: RSI(3) computed on CRSP-style back-adjusted prices operates on a continuous series. The 3-day RSI window spans the split ex-date smoothly. No artificial signal extremes at the split event. This is the REQUIRED behavior.
adjusted_prices_affecting_rsi_signal.scenario_unadjusted_prices: If RSI(3) is computed on unadjusted prices, the split ex-date appears as a massive price drop (e.g., NVDA 2024-06-10 10:1 split: $1,200 → $120 = -90% one-day 'return'). RSI would register an extreme oversold reading (RSI ~ 0) at that bar, triggering an artificial long-entry signal. This is FORBIDDEN.
adjusted_prices_affecting_rsi_signal.scenario_dividend_adjusted_prices: If RSI(3) is computed on dividend-adjusted prices (total-return series), the ex-dividend price drop is smoothed out. The signal would never see the real ex-dividend price drop that an actual trader observes. This produces a diagnostic that does not reflect tradable reality. FORBIDDEN.
adjusted_prices_affecting_rsi_signal.required_combination: Split-adjusted (backward-CRSP) AND dividend-UNADJUSTED. This combination preserves both: (i) signal continuity across splits; (ii) realistic ex-dividend price-drop visibility for RSI signal.
adjusted_prices_affecting_rsi_signal.seal_verification_required: SEAL artifact must include a worked example showing RSI(3) computation across at least one split event in the IS window for NVDA (e.g., 2021-07-20 or 2024-06-10 split ex-date region) demonstrating no artificial signal extreme.

alternatives_considered.REINVESTED: Dividend cash auto-buys additional shares of the paying stock at the ex-dividend day close. REJECTED: introduces look-ahead bias (ex-date close known only at end of day, but reinvestment would happen at ex-date open or intraday); also does not match retail cash-account reality.
alternatives_considered.IGNORED: Dividends not tracked at all in P&L. REJECTED: dividends are real cash flows; ignoring them understates total return on long positions.
alternatives_considered.CASH_ADJUSTMENT_NON_REINVESTED_SELECTED: Dividend cash credited to account on ex-date; not reinvested; tracked separately in P&L. SELECTED: matches retail cash-account reality; avoids reinvestment look-ahead bias; preserves diagnostic transparency.

dividend_treatment_specification.short_position_dividend_handling: If holding a SHORT position across ex-dividend date: the short-seller is responsible for paying the dividend to the lender. SEAL must precommit: dividend payment DEBITED from account cash on ex-date; tracked as negative dividend P&L on the short side. This is industry-standard short-borrow accounting.
dividend_treatment_specification.borrow_cost_for_shorts: Separate from dividend payment, short positions incur borrow cost (typically a small percentage per annum on shorted notional). SEAL must precommit borrow-cost estimate (typically ~0.25-1% per annum for AAPL/MSFT/NVDA at retail; verify against IBKR or Schwab's current borrow rate tables at SEAL time).

required_audits: Split event log for {AAPL, MSFT, NVDA} over IS+OOS window with cross-reference to vendor + SEC EDGAR + IR announcements
Dividend event log (regular + special) with cross-reference
Verify ZERO reverse-split events for AAPL/MSFT/NVDA in window
Verify ZERO symbol-change events for AAPL/MSFT/NVDA in window
Verify ZERO merger/acquisition completion events for AAPL/MSFT/NVDA in window
Verify ZERO spin-off events for AAPL/MSFT/NVDA in window
Verify ZERO delisting events for AAPL/MSFT/NVDA in window
Hand-check NVDA pre/post-split prices for both 2021-07-20 and 2024-06-10 splits against vendor's back-adjusted series
Verify vendor's default Close field is split-adjusted but NOT dividend-adjusted (or document reconstruction procedure)
Worked RSI(3) example across at least one NVDA split ex-date demonstrating no artificial signal extreme
Borrow-cost rate verification for short side (per requirement 16)
Survivorship-bias verbatim acknowledgment section in SEAL artifact
audit_requirements_before_seal.audit_must_complete_before_SEAL_authoriz: If ANY audit reveals a corporate-action event for AAPL/MSFT/NVDA that is not handled by this C5 extension (e.g., merger, delisting, symbol change), the candidate must be re-PLANned with a substitute universe BEFORE SEAL can be authorized.
audit_requirements_before_seal.audit_artifacts: Each audit must produce a documented artifact (event log, verification report, worked example) byte-stable in repo, referenced by SEAL artifact via sha256 anchor.

this_contract_does_NOT_authorize: Data fetch from any vendor (Databento equities / Polygon / Alpaca / IEX / yfinance / broker / exchange).
Access to DATABENTO_API_KEY or any broker credential.
Network IO.

audit_trail_disclaimer: This C5 equity-corporate-action contract extension is INFORMATIONAL AND GOVERNANCE ONLY. It defines the precommit behavior for stock splits, reverse splits, dividends, special dividends, symbol changes, mergers, spin-offs, delistings, survivorship bias acknowledgment, adjusted-vs-unadjusted OHLCV handling, vendor methodology differences, required data-vendor audit fields, RSI signal impact, and dividend treatment for the AAPL/MSFT/NVDA universe. It does NOT modify the S14-D2 PLAN, DRAFT, or any framework artifact. It does NOT authorize data fetch, SEAL, BUILD, P4, P6, P6.5, P7, P10, P11, or any phase advancement. It does NOT ratify or advance T1 (5376de7 remains byte-stable and PROVISIONAL_NOT_FULLY_RATIFIED). Operator must type the explicit SEAL authorization phrase separately to advance the candidate, AND must separately authorize the OHLCV fetch turn for the SEAL-selected vendor.
